texto: It’s not that I’m particularly worried about growing old. Nor am I all that bothered about wrinkles, grey hair and all that. BUT. Major but. I don’t like the idea of dying – not when i have so much left to do! That, people, is the rather unwelcome realisation that strikes me now and then, namely that the number of years ahead of me are fewer than the ones behind me, and while I have ticked off a lot of items on that mental list of mine, there are so many things left. Like riding through Paris in a sports car with the warm wind in my hair, to mention one.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Anna Belfrage: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “It’s not that I’m particularly worried about growing old. Nor am I all that bothered about wrinkl…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que motivacao, age, ageing pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de inspiracao e ageing.

texto: When sitting in an armchair isn't enough… I write. What I mean is that when it no longer suffices to sit and dream about travelling to other times, other places, I write about them instead. A most economical and safe form of time travel.
explicacao: Citação de Anna Belfrage sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “When sitting in an armchair isn't enough… I write. What I mean is that when it no longer suffices…” articula reflexao, writing-life, write em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como viagem e reflexao.
